message:
test(fcp-core): pin scope-intersection algebraic laws (epqsi)

Add crates/fcp-core/tests/scope_intersection_algebra.rs pinning the
four set-intersection algebraic laws asked for by the bead.

Bead asks for "CapabilityScope intersection semantics". No
CapabilityScope type exists in fcp-core, and CapabilityConstraints
has no intersect method. The only scope-bearing intersection
primitive in fcp-core is ForkEvidence::double_signers
(checkpoint.rs:281), which intersects the signer sets of two
conflicting checkpoints to identify Byzantine nodes. Signer sets are
a meaningful "scope of authority" so the four properties apply.

14 tests cover:

  1. Identity (S ∩ S = S): full set, empty set, singleton.
  2. Commutativity (A ∩ B = B ∩ A): overlapping, disjoint,
     one-side-empty.
  3. Associativity ((A ∩ B) ∩ C = A ∩ (B ∩ C)): three-way overlap,
     intersection chain through empty intermediate, full overlap.
  4. Empty-on-disjoint: disjoint sets → ∅; either side empty → ∅;
     non-disjoint → exactly the shared elements.
  5. Subset property: result ⊆ A and result ⊆ B.
  6. Determinism on repeated calls.

A regression that swapped intersection for union or difference, or
switched to a Vec-based representation that loses set semantics,
would fail at least one of these laws.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
